content: [{"section_id": "s01", "title": "Encapsulant Materials and Damp Heat Test Standards", "description": "Defines the encapsulant families (EVA, POE, TPO, PEI, polyolefin, Surlyn) and the IEC 61215/61646 damp heat test protocol (85C/85%RH, 1000-3000h). Establishes the materials-science context for adhesion degradation."}, {"section_id": "s02", "title": "Adhesion Degradation Kinetics Under Damp Heat", "description": "Synthesizes quantitative adhesion data: peel strength (N/cm), shear strength (MPa), fracture energy (kJ/m2) before and after damp heat aging. Compares EVA, POE, TPO degradation rates and Arrhenius kinetic models (activation energy, moisture-driven degradation)."}, {"section_id": "s03", "title": "Failure Mode Transitions and Interfacial Mechanisms", "description": "Examines failure mode shifts (adhesive → cohesive, interfacial debonding), hydrolytic chain scission, deacetylation in EVA, moisture ingress pathways, and the role of elastic modulus in delamination susceptibility."}, {"section_id": "s04", "title": "Formulation and Processing Parameters Affecting Durability", "description": "Covers the impact of VA content in PEVA (18-40%), gel content (65-92%), lamination temperature, and crosslinking density on damp heat resistance. Includes the 53 N/cm peel threshold for 20-year lifetime and optimal gel content ranges."}, {"section_id": "s05", "title": "Field Reliability and Industry Quality Trends", "description": "Presents PVEL scorecard data on DH pass rates (69%), failure rate doubling, cost-cutting impacts (reduced encapsulant thickness, shortened lamination), and field equivalence (10,000h accelerated = 15-20 years)."}, {"section_id": "s06", "title": "Design Implications and Research Gaps", "description": "Translates findings into material selection guidance for flexible electronics encapsulation. Identifies gaps: DVS-PEI-specific aging data, combined stress testing, long-duration field validation, and encapsulant-substrate interaction modeling."}]
